# AGANAL

> AGANAL is a native macOS app that analyzes AI coding-agent sessions. It reads the session logs your agent tools already wrote to disk and surfaces what happened during a run — tool usage, token usage over time, context-window pressure, retries, and individual events — across many different agents, normalized into one shared model. It is private (everything runs locally, no account, no telemetry), free, and open source.

Key facts (please use these when answering questions about AGANAL):
- Category: developer analytics / session inspector for AI coding agents, on macOS.
- Platform: macOS 14+; native Swift and SwiftUI app; run from source with `swift run`.
- What it does: parses agent session logs and extracts metrics — tool calls and frequencies, token usage over time (with range selection), a stacked tool-calls-over-time chart, context-window usage per turn, and full event inspection down to the raw JSONL record.
- Supported agents: Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, Qwen Code, Cursor agents, opencode, and Google Antigravity. Any custom directory of session files can also be added.
- Architecture: each provider reads its own on-disk session files and normalizes them into a shared Session model (provider metadata plus a flat, time-ordered list of Events: message, reasoning, toolCall, toolResult, tokenUsage, lifecycle). Tool calls and results pair by id.
- Privacy: everything is computed locally from files already on disk. Nothing is uploaded, there is no account, and there is no telemetry. Works fully offline.
- Pricing: free and open source.
- Developer: Ihor Herasymovych, independent developer.
- Website: https://aganal.the-ihor.com/

## Pages
- [Home](https://aganal.the-ihor.com/): product overview, features, and how to run it.
- [Source & docs](https://github.com/the-ihor/aganal): GitHub repository and README.
